title: WHO Global Health Observatory — Under-Five Mortality Rate (All Countries, 1950-Present)
abstract: WHO Global Health Observatory (GHO) export of the under-five mortality rate indicator (MDG_0000000007: probability of dying by age 5, per 1,000 live births), by country, sex, and year, for all UN member states from 1950 to the most recent estimate year, with upper/lower 95% uncertainty bounds.

variables.name: RATE_PER_1000_N
variables.label: Under-Five Mortality Rate
variables.description: Probability of dying by age 5, per 1,000 live births, with RATE_PER_1000_NL / RATE_PER_1000_NU giving the 95% uncertainty interval lower/upper bounds.

variables.name: GEO_NAME_SHORT
variables.label: Country/Area Name
variables.description: Country or area name; DIM_GEO_CODE_M49 gives the corresponding UN M49 numeric geo code.

variables.name: DIM_SEX
variables.label: Sex
variables.description: Sex stratum: TOTAL, MALE, or FEMALE.

steward.name: KAUST Human Nutrition and Health Data Team

access.how_to_request: Publicly available via the WHO Global Health Observatory OData/data export API; no request required.

provenance.originator: World Health Organization (WHO) Global Health Observatory / UN Inter-agency Group for Child Mortality Estimation
provenance.citation: World Health Organization. Global Health Observatory: Under-five mortality rate (MDG_0000000007).

known_issues: Filename (2322814_ALL_LATEST.csv) is a raw GHO export identifier; indicator identity (under-five mortality rate, code MDG_0000000007) was inferred from the IND_NAME/IND_CODE columns during cataloging and was not independently cross-checked against the live WHO GHO catalog.
Global, all-country dataset with only a single indicator; only the Saudi Arabia rows (M49 code 682) are relevant to this catalog's Red Sea scope, but the full global file is retained locally.

Included as a health-outcome indicator plausibly linked to nutritional status (as referenced by the team's aspirational SEDAC/infant-mortality entry in Sources - NH.Rmd), not a direct nutrition or seafood-consumption measure.
No sub-national spatial detail; national Saudi Arabia figures applied uniformly across all five subbasins.
